questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international organizations, which of the following best describes the membership structure of the UN Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC)?

options.text: Membership is open to all UN member states on a permanent basis.
Membership is divided among five geographic groups with seats allocated by region.
Members are appointed by the UN Secretary-General based on economic contribution.
Seats are reserved exclusively for the permanent members of the Security Council.

questions.explanation: Membership in ECOSOC is region-based and divided among five geographic groups: Africa, Asia-Pacific, Eastern European, Latin American & Caribbean, and Western European and other States. India was recently elected under the Asia-Pacific group.
questions.takeaway: ECOSOC membership is based on equitable geographical distribution across five regional groups.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Membership in ECOSOC is region-based and divided among five geographic groups: Africa, Asia-Pacific, Eastern European, Latin American & Caribbean, and Western European and other States.
